mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses equity discount rates continuously, mechanically supporting forward S&P 500 returns regardless of short-term news flow. The lower the level, the stronger the persistent tailwind on valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above an elevated threshold (8%), it signals a recessionary labor shock that has already been priced into equities; subsequent stabilization or deceleration in the rate produces a relief rally in the S&P 500 over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.8 in snapshot indicates post-shock elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns are negative or insignificant (t-test p>0.05) in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE is above 8% and decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month rebound in industrial production after a contraction signals reacceleration of real output, which forward S&P 500 prices anticipate as improving corporate earnings over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month rate of change turns positive after two consecutive negative months
failure_condition: Hit rate of positive 60d forward SPX returns following an INDPRO reacceleration signal is not above 55% over a 5-year walk-forward sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; the interaction of both rising simultaneously predicts stronger forward equity returns than either signal individually.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 and HOUST 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-signal subsample shows forward 60d SPX returns that are not statistically distinguishable from the union of single-series signals (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation combined with a federal funds rate held near zero creates a negative real interest rate regime that erodes future purchasing power expectations, causing equities to underperform over the next 5 trading days as inflation surprises get priced into risk premia.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change > 3% while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 with CPIAUCSL at 223.49 in snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are positive or flat in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth trend over the past 3 months predicts forward S&P 500 returns continuously: accelerating job creation signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, supporting equity prices over the next month regardless of the absolute employment level.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep-recession regime characterized by GDPC1 contraction and UNRATE above 8%, a stabilization in real GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change turning non-negative) signals trough formation, driving a sharp positive S&P 500 repricing over the following quarter that would not occur in a normal-growth regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ change turns >= 0 while UNRATE remains > 7%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.8 and GDPC1 at 13380.7 in snapshot suggest deep contraction regime
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following GDPC1 stabilization signals in high-unemployment regimes are not significantly higher than unconditional 60d returns (p>0.05) across at least two historical recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp threshold break where industrial production falls more than 10% year-over-year signals a severe supply-side shock; subsequent S&P 500 forward returns over the next week are negatively skewed as markets continue repricing earnings downgrades before stabilization is confirmed.
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO YoY decline exceeds -10%, or the effect is not significant at p<0.10 across historical shock episodes.
